name: Progress
description: Linear progress bar. A single visual rung — 8px tall, fully rounded — that previews how far a long-running task has advanced. The track paints with `sys.color.scrimSubtle` (the Banner-style inverse-tone scrim — ~8% black in light, ~8% white in dark) so the bar reads cleanly on any host surface tier without colliding with a fixed surface-container step. The indicator paints in `sys.color.inverseSurface` so the filled segment always contrasts against the scrim track regardless of theme. The track owns no width of its own — it stretches to fill its host column. `role='progressbar'` with `aria-valuemin / max / now` reflects the determinate value; indeterminate omits `aria-valuenow` so screen readers announce 'busy'.

value.description: Determinate value. When `max` is omitted, this is the ratio 0..1; with `max`, it is the raw count and the ratio is `value / max`. Omit (or pair with `indeterminate`) to render the indeterminate sliding animation.

max.description: Upper bound for `value`. Defaults to 1 so a `value` of 0.4 reads as 40%.

indeterminate.description: Forces indeterminate mode even when a `value` is supplied. Useful when the consumer knows the task is busy but does not have a reliable ratio yet.

aria-label.description: Accessible label describing what is in progress. Required when the bar is not paired with a visible label next to it.

track.description: Fully-rounded background block. 8px tall, `sys.color.scrimSubtle` fill (translucent inverse-tone scrim — black 8% light / white 8% dark), no stroke. Carries `role='progressbar'` and the aria-value attributes.

indicator.description: Inner filled segment painted in `sys.color.inverseSurface`. In determinate mode, translated horizontally so its trailing edge sits at the value's ratio. In indeterminate mode, a 40%-wide segment that loops from -100% to 100% on the X axis over 1.6s.

default.note: The only appearance. Track paints `sys.color.scrimSubtle` — a faint inverse-tone scrim (~8% black light / ~8% white dark) visible against every surface tier in either theme. Indicator paints in `inverseSurface` so the filled segment always contrasts against the bare track regardless of theme. Progress has no emphasis axis; if a screen needs a higher-attention progress mark, it belongs as a Banner copy nearby, not as a chromatic indicator tone.

Determinate progress carries no lifecycle states — the value is its visual state. Indeterminate is a steady animation; reduced-motion freezes it at a ~75% offset segment so the busy state stays visible.

behavior.determinateTransition: The indicator's transform transitions over 200ms `ease-out` as `value` changes, so jumps to higher ratios animate rather than snap.
behavior.indeterminateAnimation: Indicator slides from `translateX(-100%)` to `translateX(100%)` on a 1.6s loop using `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)`. Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` the animation is suppressed and the indicator settles at a steady offset segment so the busy state stays visible.
behavior.ariaContract: Container is `role='progressbar'` with `aria-valuemin=0`, `aria-valuemax=100` (determinate) or no max (indeterminate). `aria-valuenow` reflects the rounded percent in determinate mode; omitted in indeterminate so screen readers announce 'busy'.

forbidden: Progress used as a permanent zero-data placeholder — that role is `skeleton`; Progress communicates active work, not absence
indeterminate bar shown while waiting for a fetch that the host already knows is sub-300ms — flicker reads worse than a brief blank surface
Progress painted thicker than 8px — taller bars read as a UI control, not as chrome. For thick-bar metric visualisation, use a chart instead
Progress nested inside a Toast — Toast is for transient confirmations of completed actions; in-progress states belong inline on the surface that owns the work
track painted with a fixed surface-container tone (e.g. `surfaceContainerHighest`) — the track MUST be the mode-aware scrim recipe so it reads on any host surface tier
indicator painted with a chromatic primary / accent / brand tone — the family has no emphasis axis; emphasis belongs in the surrounding copy, not in the bar itself
track stroke painted with a `border:` — track is a fully-rounded block with no stroke; the affordance is the fill contrast between the scrim and the indicator
